Rubbing showing a scene from the Historic Procession of King Suryavarman II on the exterior wall of the west section of the south gallery of the temple of Angkor Wat. King Suryavarman II is seated on a throne surrounded by courtiers carrying umbrellas, fans and fly whisks, all emblems of royalty. title: Historical Gallery of Suryavarman II
highlights:
gallery, West part

## Historical Gallery of Suryavarman II Unlike of other bas-reliefs of Angkor Wat this 94 meter long military procession of Suryavarman II, who built Angkor Wat, depicts the real life of the time. Starting from the west corner the bas-relief depicts the ministers, princes and princesses on the elephants, whose rank is determined by the number of umbrellas around them. Suryavarman II and his  ...    The bas-relief starts at the left by two rows. At the top - the Royal Society and the King on the standing on a mountain Shivapada throne and stands out by its size and gilding which covered it in earlier times. At the bottom - a procession of women of the palace. Suryavarman II surrounded by umbrellas and fans gives audience. He is depicted larger than the other figures according  ...
